title: The Real Difference Between Your Choices for Solutions When PayPal and Stripe Ban Your Account: What You Need to Know
author: Unchained Commerce
excerpt: PayPal and Stripe block creators in dozens of countries. Here is the infrastructure that works everywhere they don't.

content.text: If you’re a creator in Nigeria, Pakistan, or Ghana, PayPal and Stripe aren’t just inconvenient—they’re outright banned. Gumroad and Payhip? Same story. These platforms block entire countries, not individuals. The reason? Regulatory red tape, banking instability, or perceived fraud risk. If you’re selling ebooks, courses, or templates, your options vanish overnight. Crypto-native infrastructure steps in where these platforms fail. It’s not a workaround—it’s a permanent alternative. Here’s the real comparison: what works, what doesn’t, and why.
What Each Option Actually Offers

PayPal and Stripe offer seamless integration, recurring billing, and buyer protection—if your country is approved. Gumroad and Payhip bundle hosting with payments but collapse without PayPal/Stripe. Crypto-native solutions like Unchained Commerce flip the script: no payment processor account required, no KYC, and lifetime access for a one-time fee. You get automated delivery, PDF receipts, and multi-chain payments. Tradeoff? No chargebacks or refunds. Buyers pay in crypto; you own the process. It’s raw but unblockable.
How They Handle Restricted Countries

PayPal and Stripe exclude 30+ countries outright. Even if your country is “supported,” individual accounts get flagged for KYC docs you can’t provide. Gumroad and Payhip inherit these restrictions. Crypto-native tools bypass this entirely. No country blocks. No bank account needed. Buyers scan a QR code, pay in any of 300+ coins, and get instant access. It’s not “banking the unbanked”—it’s removing banks from the equation. Permanent solution, not a temporary fix.
Payment Speed, Fees, and Real Costs

PayPal and Stripe hold funds for days, take 2.9% + $0.30 per sale, and freeze accounts without warning. Gumroad adds 8.5% + $0.30 on top. Crypto payments settle in minutes, cost 1% via NOWPayments, and never freeze. Unchained Commerce’s one-time fees ($19.99–$79.99) cover lifetime access. No subscriptions. No revenue share. Tradeoff: crypto volatility. Buyers pay in stablecoins like USDT to avoid it. You get paid faster, keep more, and never beg a support chatbot for your money.
PayPal and Stripe exclude you by design. Crypto includes you by default.
Automation and Delivery Capabilities

PayPal and Stripe require third-party tools for automated delivery. Gumroad handles it but fails if payments fail. Crypto-native systems automate everything: payment verification, file delivery, PDF receipts. Unchained Commerce’s Core Shop Engine does this out of the box. No Zapier hacks. No manual emails. Buyers pay, get their product instantly, and you’re notified. It’s not “almost as good”—it’s better for digital goods.
Which One to Choose Based on Your Situation

If your country is Stripe/PayPal-approved and you trust their terms, use them. If not, crypto-native is your only reliable option. Solo developers building decentralized stores? Start with Web3 Wallet Connect ($19.99) for frontend integration. Creators in restricted countries? Full Stack Bundle ($79.99) gives you everything. It’s not a gamble—it’s a structural shift. Banks and processors exclude you by design. Crypto includes you by default.
How do I sell digital products if PayPal is not available in my country?

If PayPal isn’t available in your country, crypto-native infrastructure is the best alternative. Platforms like Unchained Commerce let you accept 300+ cryptocurrencies without a bank account or KYC. Buyers pay via QR code, and you get instant settlement. It’s not just a PayPal replacement—it’s a system that works globally, no matter where you’re based.
One-time fees for lifetime access beat subscriptions every time.
